creator: ticso@cicely.de
text:
# fsck -p

/dev/rda0f: 10713 files, 137381 used, 46544 free (5288 frags, 5157 blocks, 2.9% fragmentation)
/dev/rda0e: FREE BLK COUNT(S) WRONG IN SUPERBLK (SALVAGED)

/dev/rda0e: 2358 files, 10826 used, 68533 free (229 frags, 8538 blocks, 0.3% fragmentation)
/dev/rda25e: 7737 files, 888629 used, 137130 free (274 frags, 17107 blocks, 0.0% fragmentation)
/dev/rda1e: 4263 files, 2753150 used, 3359 free (647 frags, 339 blocks, 0.0% fragmentation)
/dev/rda2e: 21654 files, 1773523 used, 982986 free (20978 frags, 120251 blocks, 0.8% fragmentation)
/dev/rda3e: 173492 files, 629893 used, 2126616 free (4824 frags, 265224 blocks, 0.2% fragmentation)
THE FOLLOWING FILE SYSTEMS HAD AN UNEXPECTED INCONSISTENCY:
        /dev/vinum/rd4 (/var/d4), /dev/vinum/rd3 (/var/d3), /dev/rccd0c (/var/d0)

ccd0c was not configured via ccdconfig at the time fsck was run.
With the unconfigured vinum everything seems to be OK.

How-To-Repeat: 
run fsck on an uncofigured ccd device.
